author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
“bā̐dhila māyā, jedina ha’te,
avidyā⋅moha⋅ḍore
aneka janma, labhinu āmi,
phirinu māyā⋅ghore

deva⋅dānava, mānava⋅paśu,
pataṅga kīṭa ha’ye
svarge⋅narake, bhūtale phiri,
anitya āśā la’ye

nā jāni kibā, sukṛti⋅bale,
śrīvāsa⋅suta hainu
nadīyā⋅dhāme, caraṇa tava,
daraśa paraśa kainu

sakala bāre, maraṇa⋅kāle,
aneka duḥkha pāi
tuyā prasaṅge, parama sukhe,
ebāra cale’ jāi

icchāya tora, janama jadi,
ābāra haya, hari
caraṇe tava, prema⋅bhakati,
thāke, minati kari”

jakhana śiśu, nīraba bhela,
dekhiyā prabhura līlā
śrīvāsa⋅goṣṭhī, tyajiyā śoka,
ānanda⋅magana bhelā

gaura⋅carita, amṛta⋅dhārā,
karite karite pāna
bhaktivinoda, śrīvāse māge,
jāya jena mora prāṇa
en_translation:
Since the day illusion bound me with the ropes of ignorance and delusion, I have taken many births, wandering in the whirlpool of material existence.

As a demigod, demon, human, animal, insect, or worm, I have wandered in heaven, hell, and on earth, clinging to impermanent hopes.

I do not know by what pious credits I became the son of Śrīvāsa. In Nadiyā, I saw and touched Your lotus feet.

Every time, at the moment of death, I experienced much suffering. Now, in Your company, with supreme happiness, I depart.

By Your desire, if I take birth again, O Hari, I pray that loving devotion may remain at Your feet.

When the child became silent, seeing the pastimes of the Lord, Śrīvāsa's family, abandoning grief, became absorbed in bliss.

By continuously drinking the nectarean current of Lord Caitanya's life and pastimes, Bhaktivinoda begs Śrīvāsa that his life may pass in this way.
